chunk_summary: This section addresses India-specific barriers to implementing nutrition care in chronic cardiorespiratory diseases, emphasizing localized guidelines, caregiver challenges, and structured counseling for effective delivery.
learning_objectives: Understand the barriers to implementing nutrition care in chronic cardiorespiratory diseases in India.
Learn how to adapt international and national guidelines to local contexts for conditions like CHD, CLD/BPD, and CF.
Identify caregiver-related challenges such as affordability, travel, and exhaustion in delivering prescribed nutrition plans.
Develop structured counseling strategies to improve adherence to nutrition prescriptions.
key_facts: Implementation barriers, such as cost, travel, and caregiver workload, often hinder adherence to nutrition plans in India.
For CHD with heart failure, guidelines should anchor to Indian Academy of Pediatrics standards and perioperative feeding pathways.
Preterm CLD/BPD care should combine Government of India low birth weight feeding standards with ESPGHAN fortification targets.
CF nutrition care requires ESPEN–ESPGHAN–ECFS and CFF guidelines due to specific enzyme dosing and nutrition targets.
Structured counseling should assess formula affordability, feed dilution, travel time, and caregiver exhaustion.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: For CHD with heart failure physiology, anchor fluid restriction, diuretic monitoring, and escalation thresholds to Indian Academy of Pediatrics standard treatment guidance, and pair it with pediatric cardiac intensive care nutrition pathways for perioperative feeding algorithms.
document_key_topics: Barriers to nutrition care implementation in India
Localized adaptation of international and national guidelines
Caregiver challenges in chronic cardiorespiratory disease management
Structured counseling for effective nutrition delivery
